steps.text: Click Open Console to launch the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab.
On the login page: Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit). Input the provided IAM Username and Password. Click Sign In.
Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit).
Input the provided IAM Username and Password.
Click Sign In.
In the Console, select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Challenge section 1

XYZ Company needs a secure database to store customer data for its website. Your job is to create an Amazon RDS database with MySQL, set up a security group to control access, connect to it using MySQL Workbench, and test everything. Here’s how to do each step in the simplest way:
Sign in to AWS Management Console: Log into AWS
Log into AWS
Create a Security Group for RDS Instance: Go to EC2 > Security Groups, create security group with port 3306
Go to EC2 > Security Groups, create security group with port 3306
Create RDS Database Instance: Go to RDS > Create database,pick MySQL, choose Free Tier Template andInstance:db.t3.micro,Storage:gp2
Go to RDS > Create database,
pick MySQL, choose Free Tier Template and
Instance:db.t3.micro,
Storage:gp2

Connecting to RDS Database on a DB Instance using the MySQL Workbench:Open MySQL Workbench, create a new connection, enter the RDS endpoint (from RDS console), username, password, and port 3306, then test the connection.
Open MySQL Workbench, create a new connection,
enter the RDS endpoint (from RDS console), username, password, and port 3306, then test the connection.
Check if everything works. In the lab console, click Validate to make sure the RDS database and security group are set up correctly.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console.
In the lab console, click End Challenge and wait for it to finish.
You’ve completed the RDS challenge!
